title: MxSxC1 No Particles
description:
This removes the following particles:
Buff Effects
Rain Splashes
Underwater Bubbles
body:
MxSxC1 - No Particles

This will remove the following 3 types of particles from being displayed in game:

Rain Splashes

Player Buff Effects

Underwater Bubbles

Please see screenshots (before and after) for examples.

This may offer some minimal performance increase, especially with some shaders. However, note the game is still displaying the particles and processing them with this method, it's just you can't see them.

This is particularly useful for things like screenshots, videos, competition entries, or just because you don't like the particles.

If you are looking for the other particle effects used in our videos and "The Dark Is A Cold Lonely Shadow" they will be released once finished and, along with the respective packs. The Sculk Pack is progressing slowly, and will contain a lot of the custom particles people have been asking for...

This is one of 20+ packs, and used in the following projects, none of which are ready for release.

The Dark Is A Cold Lonely Shadow

TDIACLS MxSxC1 Arena (Looking for Alpha Testers in 2024)

TDIACLS Story Mode (currently only first few chapters & "Snow Peak").

If you already have our MxSxC1WeatherPack, you may already have this included as part of that, however it will be removed from the Weather Pack in next release to give more options for turning on and off and so we can introduce replacements to these particles.

MxSxC1 Minecraft Weather Pack - Sleet - The Dark Is A Cold Lonely Shadow - No Splash Particles https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=PeEl97zjArw

Website: https://www.miscreat.co.uk/

GitHub https://github.com/MxSxC1/MxSxC1NoParticles

YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UC1emN_tByezRESsP01-EPVA

We do not create mods, we create packs and data packs within Mojang framework, which will work on Realms.

Never install any packs or mods, or anything without fully understanding what it does, and ensuring it's legitimate and safe! If anything requires elevation (which ours do not), think twice and verify!
